meta.purpose: Authority repair integration for room change, discharge, dignity, and rights-preference context after source-locked QA
meta.normalization_note: This batch adds conservative CFR anchors and review guardrails for C03, C04, D03, and D09 without displacing stronger existing direct Appendix PP mappings already verified in the app.

authority_rows.mapping_rationale_short: C03 discharge or eviction complaints align with the facility-initiated transfer and discharge protections in §483.15(c), including notice, reasons, and appeal-related safeguards.
authority_rows.human_review_note: Use C03 when the resident is being required to leave the facility or is denied return after hospitalization. NORS Table 2 still controls the code choice.

authority_rows.authority_label: Respect and dignity / room and roommate rights context
authority_rows.mapping_rationale_short: C04 room issues relate broadly to resident rights around room and roommate choice, notice of room changes, and respect for preferences while the resident remains in the facility.
authority_rows.human_review_note: This is a contextual authority bridge, not a single controlling tag. Keep C04 selection grounded in NORS definitions and use tag-level mappings only where the existing catalog already has direct support.

authority_rows.mapping_rationale_short: D03 dignity and respect complaints align with the resident's right to be treated with dignity under §483.10(a).
authority_rows.human_review_note: Use D03 alongside C03 or C04 when the complaint is also about how staff handled the move or discharge, not instead of the C-code.

authority_rows.mapping_rationale_short: D09 other rights and preferences complaints align with the self-determination and choice provisions in §483.10(f), especially when loss of choice in living arrangements is a distinct concern.
authority_rows.human_review_note: Use D09 in addition to C03 or C04 only when the resident identifies loss of choice or broader rights concerns as part of the complaint.

tooltip_rows.label: Room Move vs Discharge
tooltip_rows.short_help: A room move inside the facility is usually C04; leaving the facility is C03.
tooltip_rows.details: Use C04 for room assignment, roommate, or intra-facility room-change complaints when the resident stays in the facility. Use C03 when the resident is told to leave the facility, transferred out without proper protections, or denied return after hospitalization.

tooltip_rows.label: Related Rights Complaints
tooltip_rows.short_help: Dignity or loss-of-choice concerns may be separate complaints in the same case.
tooltip_rows.details: When a room move or discharge is handled disrespectfully, D03 may be a separate complaint. When the resident frames the problem as loss of choice, privacy, or preferences, D09 may also be relevant in addition to the main C-code.

human_review_flags.issue: The relationship between C04 room issues and §483.10(e) resident rights is broad and contextual rather than based on a single controlling Appendix PP cross-reference.
human_review_flags.recommended_action: Keep C04 grounded in NORS Table 2 and use room/roommate federal or Appendix PP references as contextual support only. Avoid implying that one F-tag conclusively controls all room-change complaints unless a specific direct mapping is already confirmed in the catalog.
